topic: ADL Lite: An Event-First Operational Ontology for Multi-Agent Concept Consensus and Knowledge Graph Authoring
target_venue: Semantic Web Journal (IOS Press, SCI Q1)
research_questions: How can event-first ontology design (Wittgenstein Tractatus §1.1) replace object-first approaches in multi-agent knowledge graph authoring?
What syntax and semantics enable LLM agents to co-author structured knowledge graphs without requiring formal ontology training?
How can consensus emerge from append-only event chains rather than mutable shared state?
Can cryptographic chain integrity replace centralized trust in multi-agent concept validation?
core_contributions: ADL Lite: a Markdown-native, four-layer language (L1 YAML Front Matter, L2 Markdown Body, L3 adl:* blocks, L4 adl:action blocks) for agentic KG authoring
Event-first operational ontology: status, confidence, and validators are computed from an append-only EventChain rather than stored as mutable fields
SSA (Structured Semantic Anchoring): typed slots that constrain the interpretive space of natural language in multi-agent settings
Multi-agent consensus protocol: provisional → validated → deprecated/forked/archived lifecycle with cryptographic chain integrity
ForkManager: divergence resolution with merge/parallel/prune strategies and isomorphism thresholding
Core ontology registry (adl_core_ontology.yaml): closed-set predicates, actions, status transitions with precondition enforcement
ActionExecutor: L4 action blocks with side-effect dispatch to external systems (Feishu/Lark bridge)
RealtimeWatcher: event-triggered pattern detection (AML laundering patterns, status transitions, frequency thresholds)
EdgeNode + SyncManager: offline-first operation with eventual consistency via timestamp-ordered merge (CRDT-like)
ADLMemory: three-tier hybrid index (Hot/Warm/Cold) with cascade filtering

key_findings: E1: Chain integrity verified — cryptographic hashes link events correctly across chains
E2: Status derivation correct — DiscoveryStatus computed from chain, not stored
E3: Snapshot roundtrip — FrontMatter ↔ EventChain consistency maintained
E4: Precondition enforcement — all Comparator types (EQ/NEQ/GT/GTE/LT/LTE/IN/EXISTS) validated
E5: 5-agent audit consensus — multi-agent validation with confidence aggregation
E6: IBM AML pipeline — 72-line CSV → event import → pattern discovery → ontology emergence

existing_artifacts.codebase: adl_lite/ (23 source files, Python 3.10+, Pydantic v2, NetworkX)
